Tests the behavior of object division and assignment with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which override the default behavior of these methods in JavaScript. The `valueOf` method returns the number `178`, and the `toString` method returns the string representation of `0`. The code then assigns this object to a new variable `copyObject`, creating a reference to the same object. An assertion checks that dividing the `object` by `2` results in the same value as dividing `copyObject` by `2`, and that the `object` is equal to `copyObject` divided by `2`. This demonstrates the use of custom object methods in arithmetic operations and the behavior of object references in JavaScript.
